title: OSIX Web
description: Design and development of OSIX corporate website with animations, dark mode and SEO optimization.
longDescription: Corporate website for our development company. Design from scratch, animations with Framer Motion, light/dark theme system, and optimization for SEO and performance.
metaDescription: OSIX Web - Corporate website with animations and dark mode

title: Nessie
description: Complete frontend development of a multi-tenant B2B platform with React, permissions system and admin panel.
longDescription: Responsible for the frontend development of Nessie, a B2B enterprise application. I implemented the complete interface: passwordless authentication system, organization and user management, super admin panel, and all application views.

caseStudy.problem: The platform needed an interface that handled the complexity of a multi-tenant system with roles, hierarchical permissions and multiple user types, while maintaining a clear and efficient UX.
caseStudy.process: I worked closely with the backend team to design user flows. I implemented reusable components and a state system that reflected the backend's organizational hierarchy.
caseStudy.solution: A modular frontend with React and Vite that consumes the REST API. State management for sessions, permissions and organization context. Responsive UI with Tailwind.
caseStudy.results: Frontend in production supporting multiple organizations with interfaces adapted according to user role.

learnings: Implemented passwordless authentication flows with magic links in the frontend
Learned to handle permissions and roles in the UI, showing/hiding features based on context
Developed complex components for managing organizational hierarchies
Improved at consuming REST APIs with token handling and automatic refresh

technical.architecture: SPA with React 19 and Vite. Routing with React Router v7. Local state with hooks. Backend communication via fetch with auth interceptors. Styled with Tailwind CSS.

highlights.title: Authentication System
highlights.description: Complete passwordless login flow: email input, waiting screen, magic link validation, and session handling with JWT.

highlights.title: Super Admin Panel
highlights.description: Complete dashboard for managing organizations, users and configuration. Tables with filters, pagination and batch actions.

highlights.title: UI Permissions Management
highlights.description: System that hides/shows elements based on user role. Route and component protection based on permissions.

challenges.challenge: Displaying different interfaces based on user role without duplicating code.
challenges.solution: HOCs and custom hooks that verify permissions. Components that receive user context and render conditionally.

challenges.challenge: Handling the hierarchy of organizational units in the UI intuitively.
challenges.solution: Expandable/collapsible tree component with drag & drop. Dynamic breadcrumbs for navigation.

challenges.challenge: Keeping the session synchronized and handling token expiration.
challenges.solution: Fetch interceptor that detects 401, attempts token refresh, and redirects to login if it fails.

featuresDetailed.name: Passwordless Login
featuresDetailed.description: Password-free authentication flow. The user enters their email and receives a magic link.
featuresDetailed.how: Email form, API call, waiting screen with instructions, and callback handler that validates the token and establishes the session.

featuresDetailed.name: Organization Dashboard
featuresDetailed.description: Main view with metrics, recent activity and quick access. Adapted according to user role.
featuresDetailed.how: Card and chart components. Data obtained from multiple endpoints and aggregated in the frontend.

featuresDetailed.name: User Management
featuresDetailed.description: Complete CRUD of users with email invitation, role assignment and status management (active/inactive).
featuresDetailed.how: Table with search, filters and pagination. Modals for create/edit. Confirmation for destructive actions.

featuresDetailed.name: Organizational Units Tree
featuresDetailed.description: Visualization and management of the hierarchy of departments, teams and projects.
featuresDetailed.how: Recursive tree component. Expand/collapse, create children, edit and delete nodes. Optimistic updates.

featuresDetailed.name: Super Admin Panel
featuresDetailed.description: Exclusive interface for super admins. Management of all organizations, limits and global configuration.
featuresDetailed.how: Protected routes with is_super_admin verification. List and detail views for organizations.

featuresDetailed.name: Notification System
featuresDetailed.description: Toasts and alerts for action feedback. API error notifications with clear messages.
featuresDetailed.how: Notification context with queue. Toast component with auto-dismiss and variants (success, error, warning).

metaDescription: Nessie - Multi-tenant B2B platform with hexagonal architecture

title: Denso
description: Minimalist study timer with Pomodoro, statistics and achievement system.
longDescription: Productivity app designed for students. Customizable Pomodoro timer, study statistics with heatmap, achievement system, task management by subject and 8 visual themes. PWA with offline support.

caseStudy.problem: Existing productivity apps are overloaded with unnecessary features. Students need something simple that works without friction and actually helps them stay focused.
caseStudy.process: I started by researching the Pomodoro technique and talking to students about their habits. I iterated the design multiple times until I achieved an interface that disappears while you study.
caseStudy.solution: A minimalist PWA that focuses on the essentials: timer, visual statistics to see your progress, and an achievement system that gamifies studying without being intrusive.
caseStudy.results: The app is actively used by students, with study sessions averaging 2+ hours per user.

learnings: Learned to design for 'non-use' — the best interface is one that disappears when you don't need it
Discovered the power of subtle gamification: achievements work better when they don't interrupt
Understood how to structure complex local state with offline persistence
Improved my understanding of performant animations in React

technical.architecture: SPA with React and Vite. Global state with Zustand persisted in localStorage. Service Worker for complete offline functionality. CSS custom properties for the theme system.

highlights.title: Dynamic Theme System
highlights.description: 8 visual themes implemented with CSS custom properties. Theme change is instant without re-render because colors are defined in :root and components inherit them.

highlights.title: Activity Heatmap
highlights.description: GitHub-style visualization of study history. Calculated in real-time from stored session data, with color interpolation based on intensity.

highlights.title: Offline-First PWA
highlights.description: Service Worker that caches all assets. The app works 100% offline — you can study on the subway without data.

challenges.challenge: The timer needed to keep running even if the user switched tabs or locked their phone.
challenges.solution: Using timestamps instead of intervals. When returning to the app, I calculate elapsed time by comparing Date.now() with the start timestamp.

challenges.challenge: Timer animations caused jank on low-end mobile devices.
challenges.solution: Optimized using will-change and transform instead of width/height. The progress circle uses CSS-animated stroke-dasharray.
Implemented a storage events listener that updates Zustand when it detects changes in localStorage from another tab.

featuresDetailed.name: Pomodoro Timer
featuresDetailed.description: Configurable timer with work and break sessions. System notifications when each phase ends.
featuresDetailed.how: Timestamp-based for precision. Uses the Notifications API for alerts. The progress circle is an SVG with animated stroke-dasharray.

featuresDetailed.name: Statistics
featuresDetailed.description: Dashboard with total study time, current streak, best streak, and monthly activity heatmap.
featuresDetailed.how: Data is aggregated in real-time from the sessions array. The heatmap uses a CSS grid with interpolated colors based on each day's study intensity.

featuresDetailed.name: Achievement System
featuresDetailed.description: 25+ unlockable achievements based on study time, streaks, and feature usage. Subtle notifications when unlocking.
featuresDetailed.how: Each achievement has conditions defined as functions. A hook checks conditions after each session and triggers notification if a new one is met.

featuresDetailed.name: Task Management
featuresDetailed.description: Task list by subject. You can mark which task you're working on during a Pomodoro session.
featuresDetailed.how: Simple CRUD with Zustand. Tasks are grouped by subject and can be filtered. The active task is associated with the session for statistics.

featuresDetailed.name: 8 Visual Themes
featuresDetailed.description: Color themes ranging from neutral to vibrant. The theme persists between sessions.
featuresDetailed.how: CSS custom properties in :root. Theme change updates variables and the entire UI updates instantly without component re-render.

featuresDetailed.name: PWA
featuresDetailed.description: Installable on mobile and desktop. Works completely offline.
featuresDetailed.how: Vite PWA plugin generates the Service Worker. Cache-first strategy for static assets. Manifest.json for installation.

metaDescription: Denso - Minimalist study timer with Pomodoro and statistics

title: Gestalt
description: Catalog platform with curated collections and product comparator.
longDescription: Curated product catalog with collection system, integrated blog, product comparator and advanced search. Minimalist design focused on the discovery experience.

caseStudy.problem: Traditional e-commerce platforms are saturated with information, pop-ups and aggressive CTAs. The user gets lost in a sea of products without context or narrative.
caseStudy.process: I studied how physical design stores curate their spaces. I applied the concept of 'collection' as a narrative unit — each collection tells a story and groups products meaningfully.
caseStudy.solution: A catalog that prioritizes discovery over immediate conversion. Editorial collections, comparator for informed decisions, and a blog that contextualizes products.
caseStudy.results: A more relaxed shopping experience where the user explores instead of being bombarded.

learnings: Understood that less friction doesn't always mean fewer steps — sometimes it means better context
Learned to design for 'browsing' instead of 'buying' — changed how I think about e-commerce
Discovered how editorial content can improve the perceived value of a product
Improved at system design: collections, products, variants, all connected

technical.architecture: SPA with React and Vite. Global state with React Context for cart and wishlist. Product data in static JSON (no backend). Client-side search with fuzzy matching.

highlights.title: Collections System
highlights.description: Each collection is an entity with its own page, editorial description, and associated products. Collections can overlap — a product can be in multiple collections.

highlights.title: Product Comparator
highlights.description: Select up to 3 products and compare them side by side. Specs align automatically and differences are visually highlighted.

highlights.title: Fuzzy Search
highlights.description: Typo-tolerant search using Fuse.js. Searches name, description, category and tags simultaneously with relevance ranking.

challenges.challenge: The comparator needed to handle products with very different specs (some have 'material', others 'capacity', etc).
challenges.solution: Normalized specs into common categories and showed only relevant ones for each comparison. Unique specs appear in an 'Other' section.

challenges.challenge: Product images had very different sizes and ratios, breaking the grid.
challenges.solution: Used object-fit: cover with fixed aspect-ratio. For detail view, implemented a lightbox that shows the full image without cropping.

challenges.challenge: Wishlist needed to persist between sessions without a backend.
challenges.solution: localStorage with a custom hook that syncs React state with storage. Includes version migration in case I change the structure.

featuresDetailed.name: Collections
featuresDetailed.description: Editorial groupings of products with their own narrative. Each collection has a dedicated page with description, hero image and product grid.
featuresDetailed.how: Many-to-many relationship between products and collections. Each collection has its own metadata (title, description, image, product order).

featuresDetailed.name: Blog
featuresDetailed.description: Articles that contextualize products — buying guides, brand stories, trends. Integrated with the catalog to link mentioned products.
featuresDetailed.how: Markdown with frontmatter for metadata. Products are referenced by ID and rendered as inline cards in the article.

featuresDetailed.name: Comparator
featuresDetailed.description: Tool to compare up to 3 products side by side. Shows aligned specs, highlighted differences and allows adding to cart from comparison.
featuresDetailed.how: Global state for products in comparison. Algorithm that finds common specs and groups them. Visual highlight for different values.

featuresDetailed.name: Search
featuresDetailed.description: Instant search with suggestions as you type. Typo-tolerant and searches multiple fields.
featuresDetailed.how: Fuse.js for client-side fuzzy search. 150ms debounce to avoid saturation. Results ranked by relevance with match highlighting.

featuresDetailed.name: Wishlist
featuresDetailed.description: List of saved products that persists between sessions. Accessible from any page with counter in header.
featuresDetailed.how: Context + localStorage. useWishlist hook that exposes add/remove/toggle/isInWishlist. Automatic sync with storage.

metaDescription: Gestalt - Catalog platform with curated collections
